name: CFSC Currency Exchange 75th & Stony Island Check Cashing and Auto License
description_short: CFSC Currency Exchange offers check cashing, money transfers, auto title/registration services, and bill payment at their 75th & Stony Island location in Chicago, open 8am-7pm weekdays.
description_long:
CFSC Currency Exchange operates a multi-service financial services location at 1611 E. 75th St in Chicago, Illinois 60649, positioning itself as an alternative to traditional banks and corporate financial institutions. The company emphasizes serving underbanked consumers who need financial services consolidated in one accessible location rather than traveling to multiple providers.

The company's core services include check cashing (personal and business checks), money orders, wire transfers, and electronic bill payment. Beyond basic check-cashing, CFSC offers currency exchange, ATM access, prepaid card services, and notably, full auto title and registration services including new vehicle registration, license plate renewals, and title transfers—services typically requiring visits to government offices. Additional services include notary public, fax services, postage stamps, coin counting, gift card redemption, and access to Cook County vital records.

CFSC distinguishes itself through operational convenience: extended hours (8am-7pm weekdays, weekend hours available), same-location handling of financial and vehicle registration needs, and direct integration with Illinois Secretary of State facilities for instant license plate processing. The company markets itself around the concept of "#GetStuffDone"—reducing consumer friction by eliminating multiple-location visits for common financial and administrative tasks.

However, as a check-cashing service rather than a bank, CFSC does not offer traditional deposit accounts, credit products, or lending services. The specific fee structure for check cashing and other services is not disclosed on their website. While the company emphasizes personalized service against corporate alternatives, consumers should verify transaction fees before using their services, as these can vary significantly in the check-cashing industry.
pros: Extended weekday hours (8am-7pm Monday-Friday) with weekend availability for working consumers
Auto title and registration services with direct Secretary of State integration—eliminates separate DMV visits
Consolidated services (check cashing, money orders, bill pay, vehicle registration, notary) in single location
Business check cashing available alongside personal check services
Foreign currency exchange services for international remittances or travel
Instant license plate processing through state facility integration
cons: No fee transparency provided on website—check-cashing fees and service charges not disclosed upfront
Limited to single Chicago location (75th & Stony Island)—no multi-branch convenience
No credit products, loans, or deposit accounts—purely transactional services
Dependent on customer bringing correct documentation for auto services (website notes requirements are 'particular')
No information on check-cashing limits, acceptance criteria, or processing times
best_for: Chicago residents without bank accounts needing check cashing and bill payment services
Vehicle owners in the 75th & Stony Island area who need title transfers or license plate renewal
Self-employed individuals and small businesses requiring business check cashing
International money transfer needs or foreign currency exchange for travel/remittances
diagnosis: CFSC is best for unbanked or underbanked Chicago residents who need consolidated check-cashing and vehicle registration services in one convenient location with extended hours. The main caveat is that without published fee schedules, consumers cannot compare costs with competitors before visiting, and the single-location format limits accessibility for those outside the 75th & Stony Island area.
services: License plate renewal and new license plate issuance
ATM service (balance inquiries and cash withdrawal)
Cook County vital records (birth, marriage, death certificates)
City vehicle stickers and license plate renewals
Coin counting and rolled coin/cash services
